PLAINTIFF PHAT TAHAI'S MOTION FOR RELIEF OF COURT ORDERED SANTIONS ISSUED ON 9/25/2007 TO DENNIS M. CARR, ESQ., IN THE AMOUNT OF $200.00, IS SCHEDULED THIS DAY. THERE BEING NO APPEARANCE NOR AN OBJECTION TENTATIVE RULING TO GRANT MOTION IS ADOPTED. MOTION IS ORDERED GRANTED. SANCTIONS ARE ORDERED VACATED. MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WITHIN FIVE DAYS. PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED. (212)
OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO DISMISS FOR DELAY IN PROSECUTION AND BRINGING THE CASE TO TRIAL FILED BY PLAINTIFF THAI, PHAT MINH

LAW AND MOTION, DEFENDANT ALIREZA CHALAKNIA'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR DELAY IN PROSECUTION AND BRINGING THE CASE TO TRIAL PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTIONS 583.410, 583.420. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR DELAY IN PROSECUTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN THAT DESPITE DILIGENT EFFORTS TO SERVE DEFENDANT, HE WAS DIFFICULT TO LOCATE DUE TO HIS USE OF ALIASES AND NUMEROUS ADDRESSES. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WAS UNABLE TO WORK FOR SOME TIME DUE TO MEDICAL ISSUES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302)

Ntc Of Mtn To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution And Bringing The Case To Trial
Off Calendar Apr-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT ALIREZA CHALAKNIA'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR DELAY IN PROSECUTION AND BRINGING THE CASE TO TRIAL PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTIONS 583.410, 583.420.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR DELAY IN PROSECUTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN THAT DESPITE DILIGENT EFFORTS TO SERVE DEFENDANT, HE WAS DIFFICULT TO LOCATE DUE TO HIS USE OF ALIASES AND NUMEROUS ADDRESSES. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WAS UNABLE TO WORK FOR SOME TIME DUE TO MEDICAL ISSUES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
